notes:
THROUGHPUT-OPTIMIZED SESSION DEFAULTS — bump panel-side server defaults to saturate the prefill+decode pipeline on M-series with 64 GB+ RAM.

User-reported: 'the current settings are a bit slow.'

WHAT'S NOW THE DEFAULT FOR NEW SESSIONS:
  Prefill Batch Size:    512 → 1024
  Prefill Step Size:    1024 → 2048
  Completion Batch Size: 512 → 1024
  Cache Memory %:         10 → 20
  Max Cache Blocks:      500 → 1000
  (already correct: continuous batching, prefix cache, paged KV cache, block disk L2 cache, stream interval 1)

Existing sessions are NOT modified — these are defaults for new sessions created after update. To apply to an existing session, open Server Settings on that session and click 'Reset all parameters to defaults.'

Memory-aware fallback preserved: Low-memory hardware still falls through to conservative LOW_MEM_CONFIG (8/32/15%). Throughput defaults assume ~64 GB+ unified memory.

GENERATION_CONFIG.JSON CONTINUES TO WORK: chat.ts reads generation_config.json from the model directory on new chat creation and applies per-chat sampling overrides (temperature, top_p, top_k, min_p, repetition_penalty) — caps temperature at 0.7 to prevent looping on low-bit JANG quants. This flow is UNTOUCHED in 1.3.99. The new server defaults are runtime engine settings (batch sizes, cache config), not sampling params.

Sister release: Swift v2.0.0-beta.15 — JANGTQ routed-bits validator (§421/§423/§425) + reasoning-parser stray-tag handling (§420). https://github.com/jjang-ai/vmlx/releases/tag/v2.0.0-beta.15

Update channels: GH release v1.3.99, latest.json 1.3.99, PyPI vmlx 1.3.99, engine __version__ 1.3.99, panel package.json 1.3.99.
